text.title: product-analytics Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for product-analytics as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Agent inventing event names (e.g., user_signed_up) when the codebase uses signup_completed.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always pass the canonical event taxonomy in the agent system prompt and reject novel event names; hallucinated event names create schema gaps that are invisible until a dashboard shows zero data.

failure-mode.description: Agent SQL joining events to users to sessions without DISTINCT, producing triple-counted metrics.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Naive joins fanout rows; require count(distinct user_id) and gate queries via sqlfluff rules, or use a metrics layer (dbt-metrics, Cube) so agents cannot write raw fanout SQL.

failure-mode.description: Agent providing a single confident cause when asked "why did retention drop?"
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Funnels and cohorts answer "what" and "where," never "why"; force the agent to list 3+ candidate causes and the disambiguating query, never a single explanation without a cited experiment.

failure-mode.description: Agent comparing partial-cohort retention to mature-cohort retention.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents will compare D30 retention of a 6-week-old cohort to D7 of a 1-week-old one; readers misread it as a retention regression. Pin window length explicitly in every prompt and chart.

failure-mode.description: Agent using Z-score against last 7 days for anomaly detection.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Z-score against last 7 days flags every product launch as an anomaly. Use a 4-week trailing baseline with seasonal adjustment.

failure-mode.description: Agent pasting 50k rows of raw event tables into context.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Do not paste 50k rows into context. Pre-aggregate to ≤200 rows or use tool-use (function calling) to query the warehouse directly.

failure-mode.description: Agent including raw event payloads (with emails, IPs, free-text) in LLM prompts.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Product events leak PII; always aggregate or hash before any LLM call; run a password-scrubber-agent pass on any data before it enters a prompt context.

failure-mode.description: Engineer shipping new events without updating tracking-plan.md.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The cron drift-linter is the only sustainable fix; agents themselves drift if not constrained to the canonical taxonomy in the system prompt.

failure-mode.description: Agent emitting chart specs against tables that do not exist.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pass the schema (or, better, a metrics-layer manifest) in the prompt and reject any chart referencing an unknown column.

failure-mode.description: A/B readouts written by agents drop confidence intervals and SRM checks.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Template the readout (the README's Analysis Report) and require explicit p, n, CI, and SRM-pass fields, or fail the report.
